prompt:
A distributed observatory ran a Byzantine-resilient sensor fusion protocol. Exactly one replica is Byzantine and exactly one non-Byzantine replica has a clock-skew fault. Replicas are A, B, C, D, E, F. The Byzantine replica can lie about any comparison involving itself, but all comparisons between non-Byzantine replicas are truthful. The clock-skewed replica is truthful but all of its local timestamps are shifted by a constant, so it appears too early in every truthful comparison that involves it. The audit log gives these derived facts:
1. Among the truthful non-skewed replicas, the event order is C before A before E before B.
2. D reports that it is before C, before A, before E, and before B.
3. F reports that B is before F, F is before E, and F is before A.
4. A, B, C, and E mutually agree on the chain C before A before E before B.
5. The Byzantine replica is the only replica whose reports force a directed cycle when combined with the mutually agreed chain.
6. The clock-skewed replica is the only non-Byzantine replica that can truthfully appear before C without contradicting the mutually agreed chain.
Identify the Byzantine replica and the clock-skewed replica. Put ONLY the ordered pair inside \boxed{} in the form Byzantine=<letter>,ClockSkew=<letter>.
